nang.name: Her
nang.note: Form, memory, longing and the beauty of the feminine.
nang.intro:
Her is the space for Nguyễn Tuấn Thịnh's paintings, sculptures and sketches of the female form.

Here the body is not approached as an object to be displayed. Through pure colour, strong contrast, the compression of form and an emphasis on curves, hands, feet and the smallest movements of the body, the woman emerges as a living presence — intimate, yet never fully graspable.

The brushwork can be forceful and the form direct, but the underlying attitude is one of tenderness and respect. The works do not close on a single reading; they leave an opening in which each viewer may meet a figure, a memory or a feeling of their own.
nang.artistNote:
I do not paint women. I paint my feeling of their world — a world I am allowed to look upon, sometimes allowed to touch, and always long to understand more fully, with all the curiosity, passion and tenderness of an artist.

Whatever form it takes, inside each of my works there is gentleness, care and reverence for the natural, deeply feminine beauty of woman — the source of every love in this world.

In my works I want to leave an invitation open, so that you may bring into them your own woman: the one you have loved or love now, the one you met somewhere in your life, the one you hope to meet — or yourself, in this very moment of your living.

When your heart stirs, that is when you know you have met “Her”.
nang.metaDescription: Paintings, sculptures and sketches of the female form, femininity, memory and feeling in the art of Nguyễn Tuấn Thịnh.

doi.name: Vibrations of Life
doi.note: Landscape, architecture, and the remembered places people have lived and passed through.
doi.intro:
Vibrations of Life gathers works about landscape, rooftops, villages, forests, streets, architecture and everyday human life.

A place in Nguyễn Tuấn Thịnh's painting is rarely a record of a location. Through simplification, compression and restructuring, familiar places gradually take on the shape of memory. A row of houses, a stretch of forest or an urban structure can hold traces of the real while becoming an interior space.

In this body of work, nature and human construction sometimes merge and sometimes stand opposed. It is the tension between them that makes the works feel at once familiar and distant.
doi.metaTitle: Vibrations of Life | Nguyễn Tuấn Thịnh
doi.metaDescription: Landscape, rooftops, forests, streets, architecture and remembered places in the work of Nguyễn Tuấn Thịnh.

hon-mang.name: Chaos & Order
hon-mang.note: Experiments in colour, structure, distortion and material.
hon-mang.intro:
Chaos & Order is the open ground for abstract and semi-abstract works, and for experiments that move beyond any settled form of expression.

Here colour, plane, mass and rhythm are released from the task of description. Form may be stretched, cut apart or almost disappear; yet behind the sense of improvisation there remains a rigorous process of organisation.

Chaos and order are not mutually exclusive states. They continually change places during the making: instinct opens the way, while experience and discipline decide where to stop.
hon-mang.metaDescription: Abstract works and experiments in colour, structure, form and material by Nguyễn Tuấn Thịnh.

home.name: Nguyễn Tuấn Thịnh
home.lead: From the human figure to landscape, architecture and abstraction, Nguyễn Tuấn Thịnh searches for the pulse of life inside colour, line and mass.
home.intro:
Nguyễn Tuấn Thịnh is a painter living and working in Hanoi. In his practice, figures, places and memories are not reproduced as static subjects; they are compressed, distorted, simplified and reorganised through colour, rhythm and direct feeling.

Pure colour, strong contrast and forceful structures of form create the visual tension in his work. Behind that force, however, remains a close and attentive way of looking: a regard for the body, for people, and for the realms of living he has passed through.
home.realmsLead: The three realms are not closed borders. They are three ways into the world of Nguyễn Tuấn Thịnh — where a single work may carry, at once, traces of the body, of daily life, of memory and of abstraction.
home.studioLead: Short notes on works in progress, on materials, tools and the rhythm of studio work; together with news of exhibitions, professional activity and milestones along the artist's path.
home.contactTitle: An encounter that begins with the work
home.contactLead: To discuss a work, propose an exhibition, pursue research or professional collaboration, or arrange a studio visit, please write through the private contact channel.

artworksOverview.title: Artworks
artworksOverview.intro:
The archive is organised around three open realms: Her, Vibrations of Life, and Chaos & Order. These are not absolute categories but routes of approach, leading the viewer into the concerns that run through the artist's practice.

You may narrow further by type, medium, period, series or theme.
artworksOverview.metaDescription: Explore the work of Nguyễn Tuấn Thịnh through three realms: Her, Vibrations of Life, and Chaos & Order.

friendsPage.title: Friends & Dialogue
friendsPage.intro:
Art does not exist only between the artist and the work. It continues in the memories, perspectives and conversations of those who have met, worked with, or walked alongside the artist.

Friends & Dialogue gathers essays, conversations, recollections and viewpoints from colleagues, researchers, collectors, models, friends and others who care about the art of Nguyễn Tuấn Thịnh.
friendsPage.disclaimer: Each contribution expresses the personal view of its author. Its presence on this website does not mean that Nguyễn Tuấn Thịnh necessarily agrees with everything it states.
friendsPage.empty: No content has been published yet.
friendsPage.metaDescription: Essays, conversations, recollections and perspectives from friends, colleagues and others engaged with the art of Nguyễn Tuấn Thịnh.

journal.title: Studio Journal · News · Exhibitions
journal.intro:
The Studio Journal records what usually lies outside the finished surface of a work: an idea taking shape, a change of composition, a material being tested, the tools that have been used, and the everyday rhythm of the artist's working life.

This is not a place where every work is explained. These are loose but honest notes on the craft — where instinct, physical effort, experience and discipline all take part in the making of a painting or a sculpture. This page also carries news of exhibitions, professional activity and events involving Nguyễn Tuấn Thịnh.
